def: s.f. Ground; site; floor; surface; bottom, underneath; foundation; depth; layer, stratum; fold, plait, ply; — real meaning or intent; hidden meaning; depth of meaning, profundity, subtleness; allusion, insinuation ( = ramz; kināya); — adj. (in comp.) Under, beneath, underground
more: n. f. 1. Surface; floor; ground; bottom. Yeh kabūtar tah kā sachchā hai . 2. Fold; plait; layer; stratum. 3. The hidden or real meaning or intent. Bāt bhī kaheṅge to tah kī. tah-bāzār . The ground or site of a market. tah-bāzārī; Pop. tah-bazārī , n. f. Ground rent of a stall in a market; a cess levied from venders at markets. tah ba tah , adv. Plait by plait; every fold. tah ba tah , adj. 1. Fold within fold; complicated; involved. 2. ( Bot .) Plicate. tah ba tah k . v. n. To pile or arrange one upon another. tah-band ; Pop. tahmad, taimat , n. m. A cloth worn round the waist like a kilt. tah-poshī , Mah. n. f. Drawers worn by women under their sārī q. v. tah-pech , n. m. A cap or any cloth worn under the turban ( batānā ). tah toṛnā, tah uṛānā , v. n. To lick the platter clean. tah jamānā , v. a. 1. To plait; pile fold on fold. 2. To drink glass upon glass.
pron. contract. of توه to-hi, q. v.

A cavern, a cellar, a room under ground, a lower story, a vault. ته دار tah-dār, adj. Having bottom or foundation or meaning, rational. ته داري tah-dār-ī, s. f. The having bottom, or foundation, or meaning. ته درز tah-darz, adj. New. ته ديگي tah-deg-ī, s. f. The scrapings of a pot, the burnt part of victuals that sti…

adj. Having bottom or foundation; having depth (lit. & fig.), deep, profound, subtle (e.g. yěh shěʻr tah-dār hai); rational; — tah-dārī, s.f. The having bottom or foundation; deepness, depth, profundity, &c.

adj. (f. -ī), Deep, profound, hidden, occult, mysterious; involved

def: n. m. A subterranean abode; a room under ground; a cellar. tah-darz ; Illit. tah-dard, tah-darad , adj. New ( nayā ); spick and span new. tah-degī , n. f. The scrapings of the pot; the burnt portion of victuals sticking to the bottom of the pot ( khurchan ). tah denā , v. n. To tinge slightly. tah k . v. a. 1. To fold up; arrange 2. To lay on the shelf; put up. 3. To settle, decide. tah kar rakkho , Fold it up; keep it for yourself; shut up. tah milānā , v. n. To pair (birds). tah-nashīn honā , v. n. To sink in the mind. tah o bālā , adv. Upside down ( ulṭā pulṭā ). tah o bālā k . v. a. To subvert; overthrow. tah ko pauṅhchnā , v. n. To get to the bottom; to find out.
s. plur. (of تحفه) 1. Rarities, curiosities. 2. Presents.

more: (inf. v. of حيرت) s. m. Amazement, wonder, astonishment.

def: s.f. Spelling; orthography
subs: s.m. The letters of the alphabet.

more: (inf. v. of هحو) s.f. Spelling. تـﮩجي کرنا tahajjī karnā, To spell. حروف تـﮩجي ḥurūf-i-tahajjī, The letters of the alphabet.

ety: inf. n. v of هجد 'to sleep'; 'to wake, watch,' &c.
def: s.m. Sleeping soundly; sleeplessness, wakefulness; a form of prayer repeated during the night.
wety: Learned borrowing from Arabic تَهَجُّد (tahajjud).

def: n. m. A form of prayer said after midnight.
more: (inf. v. of هجد) s. m. 1. A form of prayer said during the night. 2. Wakefulness. 3. Sleeping sound.

def: s. f. Pain in the joints.
s.f. Shooting pain, throb, &c. = ṭasak, q.v. s.f. Shooting or throbbing pain, dart, throb, stitch (syn. ćasak; ṭīs).

ety: inf. n. v of حق 'to be just, right, &c.'
def: s.m. Proving to be truth, reality, or fact; ascertainment, certain knowledge, certainty (cf. taḥqīq).
more: (inf. v. of حقّ) s. m. Ascertainment, certain knowledge, certainty.

ety: inf. n. v of حکم 'to restrain,' &c.

more: n. f. حکم he restrained him. See حکومت
(inf. v. of حکم) s. m. 1. Commanding, ruling, dominion, power, authority. 2. Usurping authority. تحکم کرنا taḥakkum karnā, To command, rule, &c.
subs: (-par), To exercise control or authority (over), to command, rule, &c.

def: v.n. = ṭabhakhnā and ṭasaknā, q.v.
more: v. n. 1. To be beaten, hammered, riveted, driven home. 1. Kyā kānoṅ meṅ ḍaṭṭe ṭhuk gaë? Are your ears bunged up? 2. Nālish ṭhuk gaī . The suit has been filed. 2. To be punished, chastised ( piṭnā ). Kahīṅ ṭhuk-kar āë ho? 3. To be thrown on one's back or worsted in wrestling. 4. To be defeated, overcome. 5. To meet with or suffer a loss. Das rupaë kī ṭhukī . 6. To be sent to prison, put in the stocks.

def: v. n. To pain.

ety: S. त्रख्+र; cf. ṭagharnā; ṭaghalnā
def: s.f. = ṭahal
subs: or ṭahal-ṭakor karnā (-kī), To serve, attend (on); to labour, drudge

def: ṭahal ṭakor, s. f. Housewifery, housekeeping, duty, service, task, business, work, job, use, drudgery. ٿـﮩل کرنا ṭahal karnā or ٿـﮩل ٿکور کرنا ṭahal ṭakor karnā, To serve, to drudge. ٿـﮩل لگاني ṭahal lagānī, To dance attendance.

n. f. 1. Service; attendance; servitude; duty. 1. Sādh, saṅt kī ṭahl-kar līje kuch hu dharm, Tulsī, pher nā milegā bār bār ye janm . Tulsī Dās. Serve perfect holy men, some holiness attain, This life, O Tulsi, ne'er will you times o'er regain. 2. Bābū, pahle ṭahalyā, mātā kī karī āo, Hamro ṭahil jī se bisāro . A ballad. Sarvan. Kind sir, your service first unto my mother pay, The duty which you owe to me forget, I pray. 3. Sādh saṅt kī ṭahal ko uṭho na baiṭho jāë, Tulsī, lālach len ko dauṛā dauṛā jāë . Tulsī Dās. On perfect holy men to wait they never sit or stand, By greed possessed they run and clutch whatever comes to hand. 2. Work; business; task. 3. Housewifery; house-keeping. ṭahal, yā ṭahal ṭakorī k . v. a. To serve; drudge; attend or wait upon. Phr.—To do one's bidding; to dance attendance on. 1. Ṭahal ṭikorā ne karbā to khāëlā ke detwā? Mag. If you dont work how will you live? 2. Pahle dūdh kalas bharvāyā, pāchhe terā janam karāyā, Ṭahal karan ko dās aur dāsī, sūkhe bistar tohe sulāyā . Kabir. For milk provision first was made against your natal day, Slaves for your service ready were, on bedding dry you lay.

ety: inf. n. ii of حلحل 'to remove'
def: s.m. Removing (from a place), quitting; becoming in motion.
more: (inf. ii. of حلحل) s. The being forced to move from a place.

def: n. m. ﮩلکهَ perishing. Consternation; dismay; panic.
more: (inf. i. ) s. m. 1. Ruin, perdition. 2. Consternation, alarm. 3. Pang, agony.

def: v. n. 1. To walk up and down; take a walk; rove; ramble; saunter. Savere kā ṭahalnā, din bhar kī khushī . Prov. A walk in the morning, health all the day. 2. To be off; to go away. Zarā ṭahlo to sahī . 3. To die. Woh bhī āj ṭahal gaë .
v. n. To walk backwards and forwards, to take the air, to rove, to ramble.

ety: see ṭahal
def: v.n. To walk to and fro, or up and down; to take a walk, to rove, ramble, saunter; to move off or away, to be off; to depart this life, to die
subs: v.n. To move away, to be off; to die.

def: n. f. A maid servant. Āj tohar ṭahalnī kahvāṅ gail bā je apnā hāṅthe chaukā bāsan karat bāṛū? Bhoj. Where is your servant gone that you are plastering and scrubbing with your own hands?
s. f. See ٿـﮩلوي ṭahlūʼī.

ety: from ṭahalnā
def: s.f. A woman-servant, a maid-servant ( = ṭahlūʼī); a house-wife.

ety: inf. n. vi of حلف
def: s.m. Conspiring together, leaguing; swearing (both the plaintiff and the defendant).

n. m. A servant; attendant; menial; boy. Ekrā apnā īṅhāṅ rakkhā! tohar ṭahalu hoë ke jīhī . Bhoj. Take this child! in your service he may live. (When a woman loses one or two children she makes the next child over to a neighbour in the belief that by so doing the child will live).
conj. Ped. for H. tau-bhī . Still; yet; nevertheless; not the less; notwithstanding.
conj.; see s.v. tā. s.m. A tail, the breast; the womb; the hip or flank; a warrior; a thief; a wicked man; an outcast, a barbarian; a jewel; nectar; a tree; — passing, crossing.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تا هم (tā-ham, “still, yet, nevertheless”). By surface analysis, تا (tā) + ہم (ham).

ety: inf. n. v of حمل 'to bear'
def: s.m. Enduring patiently; patience, endurance, long-suffering, resignation, forbearance; meekness, humility; truce, peace
more: n. m. حمل he bore it. Endurance. See برداشت 1.
(inf. v. of حمل) s. m. Patience, endurance, long-suffering, resignation, forbearance, truce, peace, meekness, humility. تحمل کرنا taḥammul karnā, To endure, bear, forbear, be patient.
subs: (-meṇ), To be patient or forbearing (under, or in), to bear patiently, endure, forbear.

def: adv. (Old H.) = tahāṅ, q.v.

def: adv. (correl. to jahāṅ, q.v.), There, thither; then, at that juncture

adv. contrac. of tahāṅ, q.v.
adv. S. तत्र Opp. of jahāṅ . Thither; there. jahāṅ tahāṅ , adv. Hither and thither; everywhere.
v. a. To fold, to ply, to wrap.
s. m. A branch or large bough of a tree.

ety: from tah
def: v.t. To fold, ply, wrap, &c. ( = tah karnā, q.v.s.v. tah). s.f. Plait, fold, ply ( = tah); multiplicity, perplexity.
adv. (prov.) = tahāṅ, q.v.
adv. (prov.) = tahāṅ, q.v.

ety: for A. طہارة, inf. n. of طہر 'to become clean, or pure'
def: s.f. Purity, cleanliness; sanctity; ceremonial purification; ablution, the act of performing the ablution termed g̠usl, and that termed wuẓū; and that termed istinjā
subs: To perform the ablution termed g̠usl, &c.; to wash; to purify oneself (see ābdast karnā).

def: n. f. طﮩر became pure. 1. Washing either entirely or partially. ☞ Washing the face, mouth, hands, arms, and feet, in a particular order. When this is not possible sand may be substituted, which ceremony is termed Tayammum , but ablution with water must be performed as soon as possible after the dry purification, or the latter is nullified. 2. Cleanliness. 3. Ablution; ceremonial; purification. tahārat k. , v. a. See ābdast k .
(from طﮩر Heb. and Ch. ) s. f. Purity, cleanliness, sanctity.
v.n. = ṭahalnā, q.v. v.n. To walk to and fro, or up and down; to take a walk, to rove, ramble, saunter; to move off or away, to be off; to depart this life, to die
subs: v.n. To move away, to be off; to die.

def: s.m. Becoming movent (as a letter); motion, movement; agitation.
more: (inf. v. of حرکت) s. m. Motion, movement, agitation.

def: adj. H. nās ruin. 1. Destroyed; ruined. 2. Scattered; dispersed; at sixes and sevens. tahas nahas , n. m. Destruction; ruin; subversion.

ety: inf. n. vi of حشي
def: s.m.f. Standing apart; excepting (against a thing, by saying ḥāshaʼllāh 'God forbid'); care, concern, fear (e.g. be-taḥāshā, adv. 'fearlessly').
more: (from حشي) s. 1. Exception, putting aside. 2. (perhaps, for تخاشي) Fear.

ety: prob. from S. नश, tahas being an apa-śabd
def: adj. & s.m. Destroyed, ruined; scattered, dispersed; overthrown, subverted; topsy-turvy, confused; — destruction, ruin; overthrow, subversion; dispersion; confusion.

ety: inf. n. v of حسر 'to grieve; regret'
def: s.m. Grieving (for); regretting; grief, regret ( = ḥasrat); condolence.
more: (inf. v. of حسرت) s. m. 1. Grieving, regretting, regret. 2. Condolence.

more: (inf. v. of هتک) s. m. 1. (Orig. but not used in Hind.) The being torn (a veil, &c.). 2. Disgrace, dishonour.

(inf. v. of هور) s. m. 1. Temerity, rashness, plunging inconsiderately into any business; intrepidity. 2. Fury. 3. Oppression.
(inf. v. of هوع) s. m. Sickness at stomach, inclination to vomit.

more: (inf. v. of حور) s. m. 1. Haste, celerity. 2. Anger.

more: E. n. f. Beak ( choṅch ); bill; lip ( hoṅṭ ).

n. f. S. ष्ठा place See استهان 1, 2. 1. Sītā-pat Raghu Nāth jī, tum lag merī dauṛ, Jaise kāg jahāj ko sūjhat, aur na ṭhaur . Dohā. Husband of Sītā, Raghu Nāth, on thee my final hopes are set, As on the vessel hath the wandering crow a resting place e'er met. 2. Jab Jam-rāj agampur āë, chhupat phirat kuṭhṛī kuṭhṛī, 'Nek chhimā karo, e Jam-rāj, ṭhaur ṭhaur merī māyā bikhrī! ' Bhajan. The king of death appears, the creature flies from cell to cell, 'O spare me yet a while, my scattered hoards let me but tell!' 3. Bahte ko bah jāne de, aur mat batlāë ṭhaur, Samjhāë se samjhe nahīṅ, to dhakke de de aur . Prov. Let him who will to ruin haste, no need to show the way, Who will not words of wisdom heed, just push him right away. ṭhaur rahnā , v. n. 1. To remain; stay; stand. 2. To be knocked down lifeless on the spot. Vahīṅ ṭhaur rahā. ṭhaur be ṭhaur , adv. Out of place; in the vital part. Ṭhaur…

(inf. ii. of هدّ) s. f. Terrifying, threatening, menace, threat.
(contract. of تـﮩ‍ان هين) adv. Thither, there, exactly there.

def: adv. There, in that same place, exactly there; thither, in that very direction.
more: pron. (dakh.) Thou thyself: see توهي tū-hī.

ety: act. part. of طہر 'to be clean,' &c.
def: adj. Clean, pure, unsullied, chaste.
more: (part. act. of طہر Heb. and Ch. To be pure) adj. Pure, unsullied, chaste.

more: s. f. A kind of dish, viz. Rice boiled with baṛī ( वटि ), which consists of pulse (Phaseolus radiatus) ground in a wet state, mixed with ginger, pepper, &c. and dried in small lumps.

def: n. f. A dish of rice and adaurī , q. v.; rice cooked with turmeric. Khāī Mugal kī tāhirī, ab kahāṅ jāëgī bāhrī? Prov. Your hand dip in the Mogal's dish, you cannot leave him if you wish.

ety: prob. fr. t̤āhir
def: s.f. A dish of rice and adaurī, q.v.; rice cooked with turmeric.

(inf. ii. of هيأة or هيّ) s. m. Preparation, provision, putting in order.
s.f. pl. (of next, q.v.), Salutations, &c.

more: (inf. ii. of حيّ) s. f. A salutation, congratulation, prayer, benediction. (Plur. تحيات taḥīyāt ).

def: s.m. pl. (of tahīya, q.v.), Preparatiens, arrangements.
s. m. See under ته tah.

ety: inf. n. ii of حق 'to be just, right, &c.'
def: s.f. Ascertaining, verifying the truth (of); ascertainment, inquiry, investigation, trial, verification; exactness, precision; truth, fact, certainty; — adj. Carefully ascertained or verified, well-established; authentic, true, actual, real, indubitable, sure, certain; — adv. For a fact, truly, indeed, certainly
more: n. f. حق was right. 1. Careful or close investigation; verification. 2. Exactness; certainty. tahqīq' , adj. 1. Carefully ascertained; well-established. 2. Authentic; definite. 3. Sure; correct; certain; undoubted. tahqīq' , adv. Truly; indeed; certainly. tahqīq rāë . A carefully formed opinion. tahqīq karnā , v. a. To ascertain; investigate; enquire into; make certain. be-tahqīq , adj. Not established; uncertain.

(inf. ii. of حقّ) 1. s. f. Ascertaining, ascertainment, investigation, trial, verification, exactness, precision. 2. Truth, certainty. 3. adj. Authentick, ascertained, actual, real, indubitable, apparent. 4. adv. Truly, indeed. (Plur. تحقيقات taḥḳīḳāt, Inquiry, investigation, &c.). تحقيق کرنا taḥḳīḳ karnā, To ascertain, investigate, inspect.
subs: (-kī), To make inquiry or investigation; to inquire (into), to ascertain, to investigate; to verify; to make sure (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تحقیق (tahqīq), from Arabic تَحْقِيق (taḥqīq).

def: adv. According to truth or fact; certainly.
s.f. (pl. of taḥqīq, but com. used as a sing. in Urdū), Inquiries; inquiry, investigation; verification
more: n. f. pl. of. تحقيق Investigation; enquiry; examination. tahqīqāt-i-ibtidāī, yā māqabal . Preliminary enquiry or examination. tahqīqāt karnā , v. a. To enquire; hold an enquiry; investigate.
subs: taḥqīqāt-ě-mā-qabal, s.f. Preliminary inquiry or investigation
(-kī), To inquire, &c. = taḥqīq karnā, q.v.s.v. taḥqīq.

def: adj. Sure, certain, confirmatory.
more: s. f. Accuracy: see تحقيق taḥḳīḳ.

ety: inf. n. ii of حکم
def: s.f. Putting in authority; authority; arbitration.

(inf. ii. of حقارت) s. f. Despising, scorn, neglect, disdain.

def: v. a. 1. See ṭālnā (1, 2, 3). 2. To lead; to cause to walk up and down (a horse or child). 3. To dismiss. Kaho bhaī, buḍḍhe ko ṭahlā dīyā? Slang.

s. f. A branch.

ety: dim. of ṭahnā
def: s.f. A small branch, a spray, twig.

def: s.m. Blow, thump, slap, cuff; push, shove; jog, nudge; goad, spur.

ety: inf. n. ii of حرف 'to alter in form,' &c.
def: s.f. Altering (words from their proper meanings); alteration; inversion, transposition (of a word or letter); forming an anagram; an anagram; mistranscription clerical error; deliberate alteration of a word so as t, change its meaning, falsification; — cutting the nib of a pen obliquely.
more: n. f. حرف altering. 1. Transposition of words or letters. 2. An anagram. 3. A clerical error; an alteration. 4. Tampering; falsification.
(inf. ii. of حرف) s. f. 1. Transposition of words or letters; an anagram. 2. Sharpening and cutting the point of a pen obliquely.

def: s.f. pl. (of taḥrīf), Alterations, changes; anagrams.

(inf. ii. of حرکت) s. f. 1. Movement, agitation, incitement, stimulation, excitement, instigation, temptation, encouragement. تحريک دينا taḥrīk denā, To touch, to move, to affect, to encourage, to actuate, excite. تحريک کرنا taḥrīk karnā, To tempt.
subs: taḥrīk karnā (-kī), To move, incite (to), to urge, actuate, excite, encourage, tempt; to affect, touch.

ety: for A. تحريمة taḥrīmat, fem.; from taḥrīm, inf. n. ii of حرم 'to be prohibited,' &c.

The saying allāhǒ akbar, "God is great,' at the commencement of prayer; the commencement of prayer (when the person praying is prohibited, by uttering the takbīr, from saying and doing anything extraneous to prayer); — assumption of a pilgrim's garb (since this entitles the person to reverence or respect).
more: (inf. ii. of حرم q. v.) s. m. The commencement of prayer (as, worldly thoughts are then forbidden).

ety: inf. n. ii of حر 'to be hot'; 'to be free'
def: s.f. Setting at liberty, manumission; — writing elegantly and accurately; writing, description; a written statement or declaration, a document; fee for writing (anything for another); ornamental lines (on a drawing or picture), lines; pencilling, painting (e.g. surme-kī taḥrīr); — adj. Written, dated
more: n. f. حر he wrote well. 1. A writing; a written statement or declaration; minute; composition. 2. A document; deed; a bond ( tamassuk ). 3. A fee for writing anything for another. 4. Lines; tracery. 5. Pencilling; painting. Surme kī tahrīr . Mīr Hasan.

(inf. ii. of حرّ) s. f. 1. Writing correctly, writing, description. 2. Manumission. 3. adj. Written, dated. (Plur. تحارير taḥārīr ). تحريراقليدس taḥrīr-i-uḳledas, Euclidʼs elements. تحريرکرنا taḥrīr karnā, To write, describe, record. تحريرهونا taḥrīr honā, To be written, &c.
subs: tahrīr-uqlaidas, (or simply) taḥrīr, s.f. Euclid's elements
s.f. Writing between lines; interlineation: taḥrīr-ě-z̤ahrī, s.f. A writing on the back, endorsement
(-kī), To write, to describe, to record
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تحریر (tahrīr) from Arabic تحرير (taḥrīr).

subs: v.t. To inflame, stimulate, to entice, allure. (N.B. The form in classical Arabic is taḥrīẓ, not taḥrīṣ.)

def: s. m. The stuffing of a saddle.

ety: inf. n. ii of حصل 'to be produced; to be realized'
def: s.f. Getting, acquiring; collecting; gain, acquisition, profit, attainment; learning; collection (particularly of revenues or rents); the revenue jurisdiction of a taḥṣīl-dār; the station or court of a taḥṣīl-dār
more: n. f. حصول became educated. 1. Acquisition; attainment; gain; profit. 2. Collection; collection of revenue. 3. The revenue jurisdiction of a tahsīldār or subordinate collector; a tahsīldār's station or court. tahsīl-dār' , n. m. A sub-collector of the Government. tahsīldār'ī , n. f. The office, duty, or jurisdiction of a sub-collector. tahsīl karnā, tahsīlnā , v. a. 1. To gain; get; acquire; attain. 2. To collect (revenue, etc.); realize; raise. tahsīl karne-wālā , n. m. One who collects revenue; a receiver of collections. tahsīl-i-mālguzārī, yā jamٴa . Collection of the revenue. tahsīl-i-nā-jāiz . Illegal collection of the revenue. haq-i-tahsīl . Commission or percentage on collections. khās tahsīl . Government collections. zar-i-tahsīl . Collections; amount of collections. qurq-i-tahsīl . Revenue attachment. kachchī, yā khām tahsīl . Collections direct from the cultivators. nisf tahsīl…

(inf. ii. of حصول) s. f. 1. Gain, acquisition, profit, attainment, learning. 2. Collection (particularly, of revenues). Pl. تحصيلات taḥṣīlāt, Collections, acquisitions. تحصيل کرنا taḥṣīl karnā, To acquire, to gain, to learn, to collect (revenue, &c.).
subs: s.f. The office, duty, or jurisdiction of a sub-collector; collectorship
(-kī), To get, gain, acquire; to attain, learn; to collect (revenue, &c.).

def: v.t. To acquire; to collect, gather (a tax = taḥṣīl karnā, q.v.s.v. taḥṣīl); to oppress, to afflict.

def: s.f. Rendering good, comely, or pleasing; regarding as good or beautiful, approving; approbation; applause, acclamation, cheers

ety: in A. phrases taḥtu
def: s.m. The location which is beneath, the interior part, the under part, the inferior part, depth; the nadir; — subjection, dominion, possession, authority, control; — prep. & adv. Beneath, under; in subjection (to), under the control (of, -ke)
more: n. m. تحت beneath. 1. Charge; possession; occupancy ( ikhtyār 4, 6, 7). 2. The nadir ( pattāl ). taht-us-sarāë . Ped. Lower regions. See پاتال taht meṅ , adv. Under control; in subjection to. 2. At foot; below. taht meṅ ānā , v. n. To come under one's authority. taht meṅ rakhnā , v. n. 1. To have in one's possession; to hold; have; possess. 2. To keep under; have under command; manage. taht meṅ lānā , v. a. 1. To bring under; to bring under one's authority or control; to take possession; to occupy. 2. To bring under subjection; subject; subdue.

s. m. 1. The inferiour part, below, under. 2. Subjection, possession. تحت الثرا taḥtu-s̤-s̤arā or تحت الثري taḥtu-s̤-s̤arạ̄, s. m. Under the earth. تحت الشعاع taḥtu-sh-shuʼāﻌﻌ, s. m. Change of the moon, conjunction with the sun. تحت تصرف taḥt-i-taṣarruf or تحت فرمان taḥt-i-farmān, s. m. Subjection, dominion. تحت لفظي taḥt-lafz̤ī, adj. Literal. adv. Verbally, literal translation, interlinear translation. تحت مين لانا taḥt meṅ lānā, To conquer, subjugate, bring under, reduce.
subs: s.m. The depth of the moist earth; the under part of the earth, the nether regions
s.m. Change of the moon, conjunction with the sun

s.m. & adv. Jurisdiction, control, &c. = taḥt-ě-taṣarruf, q.v.
adj. & adv. Literal, verbal (as a translation); — literally, verbally
adv. Under, under control or authority (of, -ke), in subjection (to); below, at the foot (of a page, &c.)
(-ke), To come under the authority or control (of)
v.t. To keep under, maintain in subjection, to control; to have or keep in (one's) possession, to possess, hold, have
v.t. To bring under, to reduce to subjection, to conquer, subdue; to bring under control or authority; to take possession of.

ety: Prk. थाणि or थाणे; S. स्थाने
def: postpn. (governing gen. with -ke), To, up to: (-ke taʼīṅ = ko; e.g. uske taʼiṅ = usko; apne taʼīṅ, = āp-ko or apne-āp-ko).
more: postpos. Up to, to, at. (Or denoting after a genitive form, the objective case).

def: prep. To; up to; at. It denotes the objective case, and is generally used with the possessive apne , in the sense of self, as apne taīṅ oneself.

ety: act. part. of طعن 'to pierce, stab' (with a spear, or with the tongue
def: s.m. A reviler, an asperser, a slanderer, calumniator.
more: (part. act. of طعن) s. m. An asperser, slanderer, reproacher.

ety: corr. of taʻaiyun, or of taʻyīn; but in the latter case it should be fem.
def: s.m. Appointing; appointment; deputation (see next)
more: n. m. عين appointed. Theact of appointing; determination; settlement; establishment; appointment. taaīyun k . v. n. To fix; determine; establish; institute.
(inf. v. of عين) s. m. Manifestation, appointing, appointment, establishing, establishment, assigning, deputing.
(for تعيين inf. ii. of عين) s. m. Appointing, deputation, appointment. تعين کرنا taʼīﻌn karnā, To appoint.

ety: act. part. of طير 'to fly'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Flying; — a bird, a winged creature
subs: s.m. 'The bird of the lote-tree,' a name of the angel Gabriel.

ety: inf. n. of طير 'to fly'
def: s.m. Flying; — a flying thing, a winged creature, a bird, &c. (syn. t̤āʼir).
more: s. m. 1. Flying. 2. A bird. See طاير t̤āʼịr. (Pers. plur. طيران Birds).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طَیْر (tayr), from Arabic طَيْر (ṭayr).

def: n. m. طير to fly. S. tri to cross. A bird (پرنده 1).
more: part. act. (of طير) 1. Flying. 2. s. m. A bird. (Pers. plur. طايران).

def: s. (dakh.) Clap (noise: Harris ).

ety: rt. of tairnā, q.v.+āk; Prk. आवको = S. आपि+अकः(आपकः)
def: s.m. A swimmer ( = pairāk, q.v.). s.m. A swimmer ( = tairāk).
more: n. m. A swimmer. tairā'k'ī, pairākī , n. f. The art of swimming.
wety: From تَیْرنا (tairnā, “to swim”).

ety: tairāk+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. The art of swimming ( = pairākī).

ety: tair˚ or tīr˚ = Prk. तर(इ) or तीर(इ) = S. तर(ति) or तिर(ति), rt. तॄ
def: v.n. To swim; to float (syn. pairnā); — v.t. To swim over or across; to go through, become familiar with, to master (a business, or a subject, &c.)
more: v. n. S. तरणं 1. To swim (پيرنا); to float. Tair ke āī hai bahte daryāo ko! ( Kakṛī cucumber). Delhī street cry. 2. To be well versed in; to know thoroughly. Yeh is kām meṅ khūb taire huë haiṅ .

v. a. 1. To call; call to; holla. 2. To speak; recite; sing. 3. To bawl; scream; roar. Ṭer ṭer-ke robe, apnī lāj khobe . Rus. Prov. Aloud she cries, her shame out flies. 4. To spend, pass (one's days). Umar ṭeṛ ho gaī .
v. a. To swim, to cross over.
v. (dakh.) To swim, to float ( Harris ).
subs: (-meṅ), To be well versed (in), be familiar (with).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀢𑀺𑀭𑀤𑀺 (*tiradi), from Sanskrit तरति (tarati, “crosses over”).

def: s.m. A he-goat; a buck; a stag.
more: (Heb. ) s. m. A goat (especially, an old goat).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀻𑀲 (tīsa), from Sanskrit त्रिंशत् (triṃśat).

def: adj. & adv., contrac. of next, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀻𑀲 (tīsa), from Sanskrit त्रिंशत् (triṃśat).

ety: Prk. तइसओ; S.ताट्टश+कः
def: adj. (correl. to jaisā), That-like, such as that, such; — adv. In that manner, in like manner, so; at that moment
more: Cont. of tis that, sā like. See ايسا 1. Jaise kaho, taise kareṅ . 2. Jaise ko taisā mile, jūṅ Bāman ko Nāī, Is ne kahā ashīr-bād, un ārsī kāṛh dikhāi . Prov. Diamond cut diamond, as when wily Brahman barber meets, The one his blessing gives, the other with his glass him greets. ☞ When the Brahman says ' āshīr bād ' (God be with you), it is customary to make him a present, and similarly when the barber sets his looking-glass before the other.
1. adj. Such. 2. adv. So, in that manner, in like manner, at the same moment.
subs: adv. In like manner, thus, so.

ety: Prk. तइसिल्लो; S. ताट्टश+इलः
def: A dialectic form of taisā, q.v.
adv. See s.v. taisā. adj. (correl. to jaisā), That-like, such as that, such; — adv. In that manner, in like manner, so; at that moment
subs: adv. In like manner, thus, so.

ety: inf. n. of طيش 'to be light, or inconstant,' &c.
def: s.m. Levity, folly; anger, passion, rage, choler, indignation
more: n. m. Choler; rage ( tehā ). taish meṅ ānā , v. n. To be enraged.
s. f. Levity, folly, anger, passion.
subs: or t̤aish-meṅ ānā, v.n. To become enraged, &c.

def: s. m. An amulet or charm.

ety: corr. of تعويذ taʻwīẕ?
def: s.m. A charm, amulet, a particular ornament (worn round the neck or arm) viewed generally as an amulet.

ety: prob. onomat.; cf. S. ṭiṭṭibhī
def: s.f. A sandpiper; a pewet (syn. t̤aṭīhrī).
more: s. Name of a bird. See ٿٿيـﮩري ṭaṭīhrī.

ety: see titil
def: s.m. One of the astronomical periods called karans; — deity.
adj. & s.m. Produced from partridges; — a partridge ( = tītar); a flock or a covey of partridges.
adj. Relating to the Tittiri branch of the Yajur Veda (as a student, a teacher; a section, a text, &c.).

ety: see next
def: s.m. A follower of the Tittiri branch of the Yajur Veda.

ety: intens. n. fr. طير 'to fly'
def: adj. Flying, running swiftly, fleet, spirited (horse); — s.m. A winged animal.
more: adj. (superl. of طير) Flying, running swiftly, fleet; a winged animal; (in Hindust. meaning) Ready, &c. see تيار taiyār.

def: To prepare.
adj. = P طياري t̤aiyārī, s.f. See تيار taiyār, and تياري taiyārī.

ety: for A. طيار t̤aiyār, 'sharp, quick'
def: adj. Ready, alert, willing; prepared, ready-made, finished, completed, complete; fully developed, plump, fat (as an animal, &c.); in full vigour, arrived at puberty, robust; ripened, ripe (fruit)
more: adj. 1. Ready; ready-made; prepared; willing. 1. Yūṅ kahtī sundar nār, 'suno bhartār, sej taiyār, ki dukh kachhū ghar nā', 'Piyā ham ko chhāḍ pardes-gaman nahiṅ karnā .' Bārāmāsā. 2. Dolī na kahār, bībī baiṭhī haiṅ taiyār . Prov. Nor dooly nor bearers, my lady's ready dressed. (She is not wanted and no conveyance has been sent for her). 2. Finished; completed; perfect. 3. Ripe; ripened (fruit). 4. Fully developed; plump; fat; arrived at puberty; robust. taiyār , adv. Ready ( āmādah ). taiyār k . v. a. 1. To make ready; prepare. 2. To provide; arrange; set, or put in order; make arrangements; organize. 3. To tame; train; feed up. Kabūtar taiyār karnā. taiyār h. , v. n. See āmādah h. , and āmādah , 4.
(properly طيّار) adj. Ready, prepared, finished, complete.
subs: v.t. To make or get ready, to prepare; to provide, arrange, put in order; to train; to fatten
v.n. To be ready, be prepared; to get ready, be on the alert, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تیار (tayyār), from Arabic طَيَّار (ṭayyār).

def: s.f. The state of being prepared; preparedness, readiness; preparation, getting ready, making; arrangement; show, pomp, splendour, magnificence; plumpness, fatness: robustness
more: n. f. See āmādgī . 1. Grand procession; pageantry; pomp; magnificence; splendour. Jo nau-javāṅ haiṅ unkī taiyāryāṅ baṛī haiṅ, Hātoṅ meṅ lāl chhaṛyāṅ, koṭhoṅ upar khaṛī haiṅ, Barsāt . Rains. Nazīr. What preparations great for youthful swains are made, With bright red flowers upon the housetops waits each maid. 2. Robustness; plumpness; roundness. jang kī taiyārī k . G. G. To prepare for war. saṛak kī taiyārī , G. G. n. f. Making of roads.
subs: (-kī), To make preparation (for), to prepare, get ready (for), &c.
wety: From تیار (taiyār) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. = P طيار t̤aiyār, adj. See تيار taiyār, and تياري taiyārī.

ety: v.n. fr. طيب; see t̤īb
def: adj. Good, pleasant, agreeable, sweet.
more: adj. Good, sweet, agreeable. ¶ t̤īb, s. m. 1. Perfume, odour. 2. Essence.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic طَيِّب (ṭayyib)

ety: S. त्वच्
def: s.f. The bay-tree, Laurus cassia, woody cassia; cassia bark, cinnamon.

ety: see tajnā
def: adj. & s.f. Abandoning, forsaking, giving up.
more: n. f. S. त्वच The bay tree, or its bark; Laurus cassia ; woody cassia.
s. f. The bay tree or its bark (Laurus cassia), woody cassia.
pron. (dakh.) Thee; of thee: see تجهہ tujh.

ety: A. also tāj, fr. the P.; old P. taka; Armen. thag
def: s.m. A crown, diadem, tiara; a high-crowned cap; crest, tuft, plume; comb (of a bird); — a suit in cards
more: s. m. 1. A crown, diadem, tiara. 2. A cap. 3. A crest, the comb of a bird. 4. Name of a suit in cards. The pack (گنجيفه ganjīfa ) consists of eight suits (رنگ rang ), each containing twelve circular cards, viz. a king or mīr, a wazīr, and spots from one to ten. In every suit the mīr is the highest card, and the wazīr is the second. The suits are divided into two classes: I. بيشبر Beshbar, in which, after the mīr and wazīr, the highest numbers from 10 to 1 are most powerful. These are 1. تاج tāj (a cap), 2. زرسفيد zar-i-sufaid (a silver coin or the Moon), 3. شمشير shamsher (a scimitar), 4. غلام g̠ẖulām (a slave or boy). II. کمبر Kambar, in which the lowest numbers from 1 to 10 are most powerful. These are 1. قماش ḳimāsh (representing a loaf of bread), 2. برات barāt (an oblong inscribed with characters, representing a warrant or assignment), 3. چنگ chang (a harp), 4. زرسرخ zar-i-surḵẖ (gol…
subs: s.m. A bestower of crowns, an emperor, a king
s.m. The comb of a cock; the cock's-comb flower, Amaranthus, or Celosia cristata
adj. & s.m. Crowned, diademed; crested; — a crowned head, king, sovereign
adj. & s.m. Wearing a crown, crowned; — a king, prince
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تاج (tāj).

ety: inf. n. v of جدّ 'to cut off'
def: s.m. = A تجديد tajdīd [inf. n. ii of جدّ], s.f. Renewing; renewal, novelty.
more: (inf. v. of جدّ) s. m. (from جدّ) Renewal, novelty.

ety: inf. n. vi of جہل 'to be ignorant'
def: s.m. Feigning ignorance; pretended ignorance (of), connivance; apathy, indifference
more: (inf. vi. of جـﮩل ) s. m. Pretended ignorance, connivance, apathy, indifference. تجاهل کرنا tajāhul karnā, To pretend or affect ignorance.
subs: v.n. To feign or affect ignorance.

ety: inf. n. ii of جلجل 'to move about'
def: s.m. Being moved; agitation; commotion; sinking into the ground, becoming depressed (as a foundation, &c.).
more: (inf. ii. of جلجل) s. Descending or entering into the earth, commotion.

ety: inf. n. v of جلو 'to become clear,' &c.
def: s.f. Manifestation; clearness, lustre, brightness, brilliancy, splendour, glory
more: n. f. جلو became manifest. Brightness.
(inf. v. of جلا Heb. Was manifested, &c.) s. f. Splendour, brilliancy, lustre, clearness. Plur. تجليات tajalliyāt, Lustres, illuminations.
subs: adj. Rendering bright, splendid, or glorious.

def: s.f. pl. Manifestations; lustres, illuminations; beatific visions.

ety: inf. n. v of جمل 'to be beautiful,' &c.
def: s.m. Dignity; pomp, parade; splendour, magnificence: retinue; furniture; conveniences (see next).
more: n. m. جمل was beautiful. Splendour; magnificence; pomp. Yeh tajammul, aur yeh taammul! Prov. Such parade, and such meanness!
(inf. v. of جمال) s. m. Dignity, pomp, parade, retinue, furniture, conveniences. (Plur. تجملات tajammulāt ).

def: s.m. pl. Household furniture, movables; conveniences, articles of luxury.

ety: inf. n. v of جنب 'to put at a distance'
def: s.m. Retiring, withdrawing (from), avoiding, shunning, refraining or desisting (from)
subs: (-se), To hold aloof (from), to retire, withdraw (from), to refrain (from), &c.

ety: inf. n. v of جرد 'to strip, denude'
def: s.m. Stripping or denuding oneself; cutting oneself off from society, living in solitude; solitude; celibacy.
more: (inf. v. of جرد) s. m. Living in solitude, solitude, celibacy.

ety: inf. n. v of جسّ 'to search for'
def: s.m. Searching carefully, examining, investigating, exploring, curiously prying (into), spying; search, inquiry, investigation; curiosity, inquisitiveness
more: (inf. v. of جسّ) s. m. f. Spying, exploring, search, inquiry, investigation, curiosity, inquisitiveness. تجسس کرنا tajassus karnā, To pry into, to search diligently, to explore.
subs: v.n. To search diligently, to explore; to pry (into), to play the spy, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَجَسُّس (tajassus), from Arabic تَجَسُّس (tajassus).

ety: inf. n. vi of جسر 'to be daring'
def: s.m. Carrying it high and insolently; boldness, firmness, daring, intrepidity, temerity.

ety: inf. n. vi of جوز 'to pass through or beyond'
def: s.m. Passing (beyond), surpassing; passing (over), overlooking, forgiving; departing (from), deviating, straying; exceeding (one's authority, &c.), transgressing (just bounds or limits); deviation; diversity; excess, transgression; outrage, insolence
more: n. m. جوز passing beyond. Transgression; deviation; excess; exceeding one's authority; encroachment.

(inf. vi. of جوز Heb. He departed, &c.) s. m. Departing, passing, transgressing, surpassing, excess, deviation, offending, diversity. تجاوزکرنا tajāwuz karnā, To transgress, offend, exceed, to depart (from), to pass, to surpass, to err, to deviate, to stray.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَجَاوُز (tajāwuz), borrowed from Arabic تَجَاوُز (tajāwuz), from تَجَاوَزَ (tajāwaza).

ety: corr. of A. tajazzǒ, inf. n. v of جزأ 'to divide'
def: s.f. Dividing, separating into small pieces; analysis.
more: (inf. v. of جزأ) s. f. Separating into small pieces, analysis.

ety: inf. n. ii of جدّ
def: s.f. = A تجدد tajaddud [inf. n. v of جدّ 'to cutoff'], s.m. Renewing; renewal, novelty.
more: (inf. ii. of جدّ) s. f. (from جدّ) Renewal, novelty.

ety: inf. n. ii of جہز 'to despatch, slay'
def: s.f. Fitting out, equipping; expediting, arranging, adorning; carrying to the grave, burying, burial
subs: (-kī), To fit out (an expedition, army, &c.), to equip, arrange, raise, &c.

def: (inf. ii. of جـﮩز) s. f. 1. Burial. 2. Expediting, arranging, adorning. تجـﮩبزلشکرکرنا tajʼhīz-i-lashkar karna, To raise an army.
Mah. n. f. جـﮩز he fitted out a corpse. Interment; burial.
s. m. The comb of a cock; the cockscomb flower (Amaranthus, or Celosia).

ety: inf. n. ii of عجل 'to make haste,' &c.
def: s.f. Hastening; haste, despatch, expedition; speediness, agility.
more: (inf. ii. of عجل) s. f. Agility, haste, despatch, expedition.

ety: act. part. of تجر 'to traffic'
def: s.m. A merchant, trader.
more: part. act. (of تجارت used substantively) A merchant, trader.

def: n. m. تاجر compensating. A merchant ( saudāgar ); a trader.
(tad+jṅa), adj. & s.m. lit. 'Knowing that'; knowing, experienced (in), familiar (with); a knowing or intelligent man, a connoiseur.

ety: S. त्यजनीयं, rt. त्यज्
def: v.t. To abandon, give up, relinquish, quit, leave, forsake, desert
more: v. a. S. त्यजन 1. To relinquish; quit; leave; abandon; desert; forsake ( chhoṛnā ). 2. To disclaim; renounce; abjure. 3. To repudiate; divorce (a wife). 4. To abdicate.
v. a. To abandon, to desert, to quit, to leave.

def: adj. = tattwa-jnā, q.v.s.v. tattwa.

ety: inf. n. ii of جنّس jannasa, a denom. from jins, q.v.
def: s.f. Making of the same kind, making homogeneous (with); resemblance, analogy; (in Phet.) alliteration; punning (especially to the eye); pun; equivoque
more: n. f. جنس made homogeneous. 1. Resemblance; analogy. 2. Punning to the eye; alliteration. tajnīs-i-khattī . (In the Persian character) Words having the same characters which vary with different vowels or points.

(inf. ii. of جنس Ch. Kind) s. f. 1. Making of the same kind; resemblance, analogy. 2. Equivocation. 3. Punning to the eye instead of the ear, alliteration. تجنيس خطي tajnīs-i-khat̤t̤ī, Writing words alike which are pronounced differently, a sort of equivoque in writing; as pul, pal and pil in this couplet of Saudā: يـﮩـ نـﮩودريا که جس سي گذريي پل باندهکر موج چشم عاشقان دي توڙپل مين پل کي پل yih na ho daryā ki jis se guẕriye pul bāndhkar; mauj-i-chashm-i-āﻌshiḳāṅ de toṛ pal meṅ pil ke pul, This is not a river that you can cross by means of a bridge; the waves that arise from the eyes of lovers would demolish a bridge in a moment by their shock.
subs: s.f. Using words which are written with the same letters but with different vowel points, a kind of equivoque in writing (e.g. pal, pil, pul in the following verse of Saudā's
mauj-ě-ćashm-ě-ʻāshiqān de toṛ pal-meṅ pil-ke pul
'the waves from the eyes of lovers would in a moment demolish a bridge by their shock').

def: To try, prove, assay.

ety: for A. تجربة tajribat, fem.; inf. n. ii of جرب 'to have the mange'

more: n. m. جرب he made trial of. Trial; experiment; test. tajrabā-kār , adj. 1. Experienced; veteran. 2. Versed; conversant; expert; skilful. tajrabā-kārī , n. f. Experience; practical knowledge.

(inf. ii. of جرب) s. m. Experiment, trial, proof, experience, assay, probation. تجربه کار tajriba-kār, adj. Expert, experienced, conversant in. تجربه کاري tajriba-kār-ī, s. f. Experience. تجربه کرنا tajriba karnā, v. a. To experience.
subs: adj. Experienced, expert, versed (in), conversant (in), skilful; — tajriba-kārī, s.f. Experience, practical knowledge
(-kā), To make trial (of), to try, prove, assay; to experience.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تجربه (tajriba, tajruba), from Arabic تَجْرِبَة (tajriba).

def: s.f. = next, q.v.

def: adj. & s.m. See s.v. ṭāj. s.m. A crown, diadem, tiara; a high-crowned cap; crest, tuft, plume; comb (of a bird); — a suit in cards

adj. & s.m. Wearing a crown, crowned; — a king, prince

ety: inf. n. ii of جوف 'Being hollow'
def: s.f. Hollowing out, making concave.
more: (inf. ii. of جوف) s. f. Hollowing, making concave.

ety: inf. n. ii of جوز 'to pass through,' &c.
def: s.f. Permitting, allowing; approving; permission; approbation; inquiring into, examining, considering; inquiry, investigation, consideration, deliberation; view, opinion, judgment; estimate; resolution, determination; trial; decision, judgment, sentence; contrivance, scheme, plan, device
more: n. m. جوز he made or held it lawful. 1. Deliberation; view; opinion; judgment. 2. Plan. See ( upāë 1, 2, 4). 3. Estimate ( bichār , 7). 4. Resolution; judgment ( bichār , 8). tajvīz-i-akhīr . A final judgment. tajvīz be-tahqīq karnā , v. a. To predetermine; forejudge; prejudge; pronounce at random. tajvīz-i-sānī yā jadīd . Retrial; new trial; fresh trial; revision; review of order or judgment. tajvīz sabūt-i-jurm . Determination of guilt. tajvīz k . v. a. See bichār'nā , 3, 7, 8. & upāë k . 1. To determine; decide; resolve; conclude; rule; establish. 2. To pronounce or give judgment; to adjudge; award. 3. To elect; choose; nominate. az rāh-i-tajvīz , adv. Judicially.

(inf. ii. of جوز Heb. Passed, removed) s. f. 1. Approbation, permission. 2. Inquiring into, examining, investigation, considering. 3. Contrivance, sentence, judgment, determination, trial, scheme, plan. تجويز کرنا tajwīz karnā, To examine, investigate, inquire into, to contrive, to plan, to sentence or pass sentence, to prescribe, appoint. تجويزهونا tajwīz honā, To be examined, contrived, &c.
s. f. Deliberation: device. تجويزکرنا tajwīz karnā, To devise.
subs: tajwīz-ě-jadīd, s.f. Another or fresh trial, re-trial; revision; review of a decision or judgment
adj. Requiring to be determined or decided; for adjudication or decision, to be tried
(-kī), To inquire (into), to examine, investigate; to determine, decide; to pass sentence, pronounce judgment; to choose, elect; to prescribe, appoint; to contrive, devise, plan
v.n. To be mquired (into), be investigated, &c.

def: adj. Tried, proved, determined.

ety: S. द्राक्षा
def: s.f. A vine; creeper; branch of any tree growing like a vine; grapes.

ety: S. दघ्न; or perhaps a contrac. of tā+lag; cf. talak
def: postpn. To, toward, up to, near to, even to, as far as, so much as, to the extent (of), including, with; — till, until, as long as, while, during, for.
more: n. f. A large scale for weighing heavy goods.
adv. Till; to; up to; until; while. kisī miyād tak , G. G. For a term; for a given time or period.
n. f. Sight; a look; stare.
1. adv. Till, to, up to, while, toward, near to, with. 2. s. f. A scale, balance. 3. verbal n. f. (from تکنا) Aim. تک باندهنا يا رهنا يالگانا tak-bāndhnā or -rahnā or -lagānā, To gaze, to stare.

def: s.f. Look, glance; aim, &c. ( = tāk, q.v.)
subs: tak lagānā (-par) = tāk bāṅdhnā, &c., q.v.s.v. tāk.

ety: Prk. तक्का; S. तर्का
def: s.f. A balance, a scale (esp. a large one for weighing heavy goods).
s.f. Sight, look, gaze, stare, &c. ( = tak and tāk, q.v.); temper, nature, disposition (in these senses probably akin to S. sthā)
more: s. Temper, nature, disposition. ٿک باندهنا ṭak bāndhnā, To stare, to gaze. ٿک لگانا ṭak lagānā, To expect, to long for.
subs: adv. With fixed or intent look, with steadfast gaze
and ṭak lagānā, see s.v. tak or tāk.

ety: Ar. form of old P. تاك tāk; and of Pehl. tāk = P. tāh or tā, q.v.
def: s.m. An arched building; an arch; a cupola, vault; a recess (in a wall), a niche; a shelf; a projecting part, a cornice, a ledge, &c.; a window; a balcony; — adj. & s.m. Single, sole; uneven, odd (opp. to juft); singular, rare, unique, unmatched, unequalled, unrivalled; — a fold, plait, ply
more: n. m. 1. An arch. 2. A recess in a wall; niche. Uṛṭī urṭī tāq baiṭhī . Prov. The report is confirmed. 2. An odd number. Dekh lo tāq nahīn juft . tāq bharnā , Mah. Wom. v. a. To offer up a lighted lamp in a mosque as a votive offering. tāq par rakhnā , v. a. 1. To lay on the shelf. 1. Bed Purān sab hī paḍhe, dharīṅ kitābeṅ tāq, Prem achchhar jāne nahīṅ, paṛho likho sab khāk . Veds and Purans he read, and laid upon the shelf, Love's alphabet unlearnt, his learning all was pelf. 2. Rakho tāq par apnī yeh dostī, Na dushman merā ek zamānā karol Zafar. 2. To pigeonhole; put aside ( chhappar par rakhnā .) tāq par h., yā rakkhā rahnā , v. n. To be rendered ineffective or useless. Agar hākim phir gayā to sārī tadbīreṅ tāq par rakkhī raheṅgī . tāq-juft . The game of odd and even.
adj. Unequalled; unrivalled; A 1 ( ikkā ). Woh har fan meṅ tāq hai .

1. s. m. An arched building, arch, cupola. 2. A recess in a wall, shelf, niche. 3. A window. 4. adj. Unique, singular. 5. Odd (not even). 6. Fold, ply. طاق پررکهنا t̤āḳ par rakhnā (to lay on the shelf) 1. To refuse, to disobey. 2. To neglect, to abandon. طاق پرهونا t̤āḳ par honā, or طاق هونا t̤āḳ honā, To be laid on the shelf, to be thrown aside. طاق جفث t̤āḳ-juft, s. m. The game of odd or even.
subs: (-kā), To place a lighted lamp as a votive offering in a niche (in a mosque, &c.)
v.n. 'To settle on a ledge,' &c.; to become an accomplished fact; to be brought home (to); to be confirmed (as a report, e.g. urṭī urṭī t̤āq baiṭhī, 'the report is confirmed')
(-ko), To lay on the shelf; to put or set aside; to neglect, abandon; to refuse; to disobey
v.n. To be laid on the shelf, to be set or thrown aside; to be rendered ineffective or useless
s.m. The game of odd or even.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طاق (tâq), ultimately from Middle Persian [script needed] (tʾg /⁠tāg⁠/, “arch”).

ety: v.n. fr. tāknā, q.v.
def: s.f. Look, regard, glance, gaze; fixed look, stare; peep, watch, look-out, expectation; view, aim, the act of aiming (at); pointing (of a dog, &c.)
more: n. f. S. तर्क 1. A fixed look; stare; watch. 1. Piyāre, jo tum ko shauq na thā tāk jhāṅk kā, Rakkhe haiṅ phir ye rozan-i-dīvār kis liye? My love, if you'd no wish to peep and look without, Why then have you this hole here in the wall cut out? 2. Ābat dekho sant ko, mūṅh līno jhaṭ ḍhāṅk, Tulsī, beswā dekhat hī karan lage tāk jhāṅk . Dohā. See where a saint doth come—he quickly turns and hides his face— Tulsī, he hangs about and peers to meet a harlot base. 2. Aim; direction; pointing (dog or cat). tākā-tākī , n. f. A mutual staring; bo-peep.
s. f. 1. Look, fixed regard, glance, peep. 2. Aim, view, the act of aiming, pointing (of a dog). تاک باندهنا tāk bāndhnā, To covet, to point (as a dog). تاک رهنا tāk rahnā, To aspire.
s. f. A vine; branch of any tree growing or trained like a vine, grapes.
subs: s.f. Mutual gazing or staring, exchange of glances; bo-peep
s.f. Precise moment, nick of time
(-par), To fix the gaze (on), look fixedly (at); to take aim (at); to point (as a dog, &c.); to look longingly (at), to covet
(-kī), To come in expectation (of), to come with an eye (to)
tāk-meṅ rahnā (-kī), To be on the look out or watch (for), to lie in wait (for).

ety: Prk. टक्कओ; S. टङ्ककः
def: s.m. A ready, pert, or plain answer; a point-blank refusal
more: s. m. A copper coin equal to two paisā.
subs: s.f. A sole individual life without relations or dependants
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi ٿکا (ṭka /⁠ṭakā⁠/) (c. 1639), derived from Sanskrit टङ्क (ṭaṅka). Sense 1 is a semantic loan from Bengali টাকা (ṭaka). Cognate with Gujarati ટકો (ṭako).

ety: for A. طاقة, n. of un. fr. t̤āq, q.v.
def: s.m. A single piece; a piece (of woollen cloth, &c.).
more: n. m. A piece of silk or woollen cloth.

ety: inf. n. v. of کبر 'to be great'
def: s.m. Pride, haughtiness, arrogance, loftiness, presumption, grandeur.
more: n. m. کبر being proud. Hauteur. See ابهمان
(inf. v. of کبر) s. m. Arrogance, haughtiness, pride.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَكَبُّر (takabbur).

def: s.f. = takabbur, q.v-

ety: inf. n. v of قيض 'to take'
def: s.m. Mutual surrender, silent exchange of an article and its cost.

ety: inf. n. vi of قبل 'to come forward, to approach,' &c.
def: s.m. Encountering, standing face to face, the being drawn up in mutual opposition.
more: (inf. vi. of قبل) s. m. Encountering, the being drawn up in mutual opposition.

ety: 3rd pers. sing. perf. act. of conj. v. of قدس 'to be pure or holy'
def: adj. Sanctified, pure, holy (said of God).
more: (3d pers. sing. past tense of v. conjug. from قدس used adjectively) Pure, holy, purified. تقدس تعاليٰٰ taḳaddasa taʼāﻌlạ̄ (an epithet of the deity) Holy most high.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقَدُّس (taqaddus), borrowed from Arabic تَقَدُّس (taqaddus).

def: s. (dakh. apparently for تکدد takaddud, q. v.) s. Labour (daily: Harris ).

ety: inf. n. v of کدّ 'to labour'
def: s.m. Labour, toil; hardship. (Not classical Arabic.)
more: (inf. v. of کدّ) s. Toil, labour.

ety: inf. n. v of قدم 'to precede'
def: s.m. Preceding; priority, precedence, pre-eminence.
more: n. m. قدم Priority; pre- eminence.
(inf. v. of قدم) s. m. Priority.

ety: inf. n. v of کدر 'to be turbid,' &c.
def: s.m. Being turbid; being disturbed or troubled (in mind, &c.); muddiness, turbidness; dregs; dulness; disturbed state (of mind), disquietude; sullenness, moroseness.
more: (inf. v. of کدر) s. m. Muddiness, turbidness, dregs; dulness, moroseness, sullenness, disquietude of mind.

ety: inf. n. v of قدس
def: s.m. Being pure and holy; purity, sanctity, holiness.
more: (inf. v. of قدس) s. m. Purity, sanctity, holiness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقَدُّس (taqaddus), borrowed from Arabic تَقَدُّس (taqaddus).

ety: inf. n. vi of قدم 'to precede,' &c.
def: s.m. Being ancient, antique or old; preceding, going before; (in law) such a distance of time as suffices to prevent punishment (similar to the 'statutory limitations' of English law).

ety: inf. n. v of کفل 'to be security (for)'
def: s.m. Taking security; becoming security for another; surety; bail.
more: (inf. v. of کفل) s. m. 1. Taking security. 2. Becoming security for another; bail.

ety: inf. n. vi of کہل 'to be slow,' &c.
def: s.m. Indolence, negligence, remissness.
more: (inf. vi. of کـﮩل) s. m. Negligence, remissness.

def: s. f. 1. A tax, duty, collection, imposition. 2. A cheap trull.

ety: from ṭakā
def: s.f. A tax, duty, collection, imposition; a cheap trull ( = ṭake-wālī).

adj. H. ṭakā , q. v. Rich; possessing ready money. ṭakait-rāë, ṭakait-chand , n. m. A wealthy person. ṭakait rāë kā sālā, yā beṭā , n. m. ( Slang ). A brother-in-law or son of Takait Rāë (a rich man).

ety: S. टङ्क+Prk. आइत्तो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन्
def: adj. & s.m. Possessing ready money, moneyed, rich, wealthy
more: (from ٿکا) adj. Rich, possessing ready money.
subs: ṭakait-rāʼe, s.m. A moneyed man (com. as a nickname).

ety: S. टङ्क+Prk. आइत्तो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन्
def: adj. & s.m. Possessing ready money, moneyed, rich, wealthy
more: (from ٿکا) adj. Rich, possessing ready money.
subs: ṭakait-rāʼe, s.m. A moneyed man (com. as a nickname).

ety: inf. n. v of قيد 'to be bound'
def: s.f. (m.?), Application, attention, diligence, industry, assiduity, care; strictness; overseeing, superintending, oversight, superintendence, surveillance, observation, vigilance; enjoining, injunction
more: n. f. قيد restraining; See تاکيد 1. Looking after; observation; watchfulness; vigilance. taqaiyud k . v. n. To look closely after.
(inf. v. of قيد) s. m. f. 1. Application, attention, diligence, industry, strictness, injunction. 2. Care, assiduity, superintending, overseeing. (Plur. تقيدات).
subs: (-par), To superintend, look closely (after); to impress (upon), enjoin, caution, &c.

def: s.f. pl. (of taqaiyud), Injunctions, cautions, &c.
s.f. pl. (of taklīf), Impositions, difficulties, distresses, hardships, trials.
more: s. plur. (of تکليف) Impositions, distresses, difficulties.

ety: inf. n. v of قلب 'to turn; to invert,' &c.
def: s.m. Being turned, being inverted; turn, change, inversion, conversion, &c. (see the trans. form taqlīb).
more: (inf. v. of قلب) s. m. Conversion, inversion, change, transmutation.

def: s.m. pl. (of taqallub), Turns, changes, &c.

ety: inf. n. v of کلف 'to attend carefully (to)'
def: s.m. Taking (anything) upon oneself gratuitously or without being required to do it, gratuitousness; taking much pains personally (in any matter); pains, attention, industry, perseverance; trouble, inconvenience; elaborate preparation (for); profusion, extravagance; careful observance of etiquette, ceremony, formality; dissimulation, insincerity
more: n. m. کلف 1. Ceremony; formality. 2. Forms of ceremony; strict observance of the rules of etiquette; elaborate preparation for one's reception. be-takalluf , adj. 1. Without ceremony or formality. 2. Plain; not elaborate. 3. Unceremonious; familiar. 1. Takalluf meṅ taklīf . Prov. In ceremony trouble. 2. Takalluf meṅ rel chal dī . Prov. While ceremony squabbles the train is off. ☞ Two punctilious Native gentlemen on the platform of the Railway station at Lucknow outdo each other in politeness— 'Hazrat savār hūjīye!' 'Qiblā, āp!' 'Nahīṅ, vallah, āp!' Be seated, your highness!' 'After your reverence!' 'No, by Heaven, after your excellency!' (The train was off) .

(inf. v. of کلف) s. m. 1. Inconvenience, ceremony, trouble, taking much pains oneʼs self in any matter. 2. Extravagance, profusion. 3. Preparation. (Plur. تکلفات takallufāt ). تکلفات دربار takallufāt-i-darbār, Expending much. تکلفات رسمي takallufāt-i-rasmī, Ceremonious attention, empty or formal compliments. تکلفات مجلس takallufāt-i-majlis, The ceremonies of society, etiquette. تکلف برطرف takalluf bar-t̤araf, Waving ceremony. تکلف کرنا takalluf karnā, To take pains, to be ceremonious (in). تکلف مزاج takalluf-mizāj, adj. Ceremonious.
subs: adj. On which great pains have been bestowed, laboured, elaborate, high-wrought, finished, elegant
(-meṅ), To take great pains (in); to be ceremonious (in), &c.

ety: of takalluf
def: s.m. Ceremonious attentions, formal or empty compliments
subs: s.m. The ceremonies of society, etiquette.

ety: inf. n. v of کلم; see kalām
def: s.m. Speaking, talking; speech, conversation.
more: (inf. v. of کلم) s. m. Conversation.

ety: fr. S. तक
def: s.m. Motion, agitation; gesture
more: n. f. Fatigue; lassitude; weariness. takān yā thakan chaṛhnā , v. n. To be fatigued; tired. Aisī thakan chaṛhī, ṭāṅgeṅ amal gaīṅ .
subs: v.t. To put in motion, to shake, jolt; to agitate; to brush off.

def: s.f. = thakān, q.v.
s. (dakh.) A pony (of a better sort, Harris ): see ٿانگهن ṭānghan.

ety: takā˚ = Prk. तक्काव˚ or तक्कावे˚ = S. तर्क+आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To aim.
more: v. a. 1. To stop; retain; fix in any place. 2. To lodge; station; billet. Uprī musāphir ke ke ṭikāve? Mag. 3. To beat; strike. Ek thappaṛ ṭikāyā .
(trans. of تکنا q. v.) v. a. To aim.

def: s.f. = tag-ā-pū, q.v.s.v. tag. s.m. The poplar; the elm; the tamarisk; a pomegranate; an ensign-staff.
s.f. = tag-ā-pū, q.v.s.v. tag. s.m. The poplar; the elm; the tamarisk; a pomegranate; an ensign-staff.

ety: inf. n. v of قرب 'to draw near,'
def: s.m. Striving to draw near, seeking admittance; nearness, propinquity, approximation, approach; access (to a monarch or to the Deity)
more: (inf. v. of قرب) s. m. Propinquity, nearness, approximation, access; approach or access to God.
subs: (-kā), To obtain access or admittance (to a monarch, &c.).

ety: inf. n. v of قرّ 'to remain firm, to rest,' &c.
def: s.m. Being established; confirmation, ratification; settlement; appointment; approbation; appropriation
more: n. m. قر remaining firm, resting. See taqarrurī , 2.
(inf. v. of قرار) s. m. Confirmation, ratification, approbation, appropriation.

ety: inf. n. v of کرّ
def: s.m. Repeating, reiterating, repetition; persevering.
more: (inf. v. of کرّ) s. m. Repetition, repeating, reiterating, persevering.

def: s.f. Settlement; appointment ( = taqarrur).
more: n. f. 1. Settlement. 2. The act of appointing; nomination; appointment. parvāna-i-taqar'rurī , n. f. A letter of appointment.

ety: inf. n. vi of قرب 'to draw near'
def: s.m. Approaching one another; name of a measure (in versification).
more: (inf. vi. of قرب) s. m. 1. Approaching one another. 2. (in versification) Name of a measure.

ety: inf. n. v. of قسف 'to be squalid,' &c.
def: s.m. Living poorly; being scantily fed and coarsely clothed.
(inf. v. of قشف) s. The being clothed in poor garments and subsisting on scanty food.

ety: inf. n. v of کسل 'to be sluggish'
def: s.m. Being lazy or inactive; inactivity, want of energy; indisposition, sickness. (Not classical Arabic.)
more: (inf. of کسل) s. m. Sickness, being lazy or inactive, indisposition.

ety: inf. n. v of کسر 'to break'
def: s.m. Being broken or shattered; being spent; being debilitated; carrying (figures in arithmetic); folding up (the wings, as a bird when about to cease from flight).
more: (inf. v. of کسر) s. m. 1. Being broken or shattered, spent or debilitated. 2. Carrying (figures, in arithmetick). 3. Folding up the wings (as a bird when about to cease from flight).

ety: inf. n. vi of کسل 'to be torpid or sluggish'
def: s.m. Indolence, sluggishness; negligence, carelessness.
more: (inf. vi. of کسل) s. m. Indolence, negligence, carelessness.

ety: for A. طاقة, fr. اطاقة it̤āqat, inf. n. of iv of طوق
def: s.f. Ability to accomplish, capability; ability, power, energy, force, strength; ability to endure, power of endurance, endurance, patience
more: n. f. طوق able to do. 1. Might; means ( bal , 7). Tāqat-ī-mehmāṅ na dāsht, khāna bā mehmāṅ guzāsht . P. Prov. To treat your guest unable, to leave the house you're able. 2. Capability; ability; faculty; energy. tāqat-i-badanī, yā jīsmānī . Bodily strength. tāqat tāq h. , v. n. To lose one's strength. tāqat k. , v. a. To exert oneself; to use one's strength. tāqat-var , adj. Powerful ( zorāvar ).
s. f. Strength, power, force, patience, ability. طاقت آزماٴي t̤āḳat-āzmā-ī, s. f. Trial of strength. طاقت سي طاق هونا t̤āḳat se t̤āḳ honā, To be weak, feeble, powerless.
subs: s.f. Trial of ability, or of strength, &c.
t̤āqat-se t̤āq honā or t̤āqat t̤āq honā
v.n. To be deprived of strength, &c.; to lose strength, to be or become feeble or powerless
v.n. To exercise or put forth strength, to exert oneself (in, -meṅ)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طَاقَت (tāqat). First attested in c. 1599 as Middle Hindi طاقت (t̤aqt).

ety: inf. n. vi of قطع 'to cut off,' &c.
def: s.m. Being disunited; being detached or placed separately; cutting one another, intersection (of lines)
subs: v.n. To cut one another, to intersect; — v.t. To cut off (from, -se), to detach (from); to place separately or loosely (upon, -par); to give (one) the cut.

ety: inf. n. vi of قطر 'to drop'
def: s.m. Dropping continuously; distilling drop by drop; raining.
more: (inf. vi. of قطر) s. m. 1. Distilling drop by drop. 2. Raining.
